questions.question: What is the purpose of generics in Java?
options: Type-safe code that works with different types without casting
questions.answer: Type-safe code that works with different types without casting

questions.question: What does `<T>` represent in generics?
options: A placeholder for a type specified at use
questions.answer: A placeholder for a type specified at use

questions.question: What is the diamond operator `<>`?
options: Type inference — compiler deduces the generic type from context (Java 7+)
questions.answer: Type inference — compiler deduces the generic type from context (Java 7+)

questions.question: When are generic type parameters checked?
questions.answer: At compile time

questions.question: What is type erasure?
options: Generic type parameters are removed at compile time — not available at runtime
questions.answer: Generic type parameters are removed at compile time — not available at runtime

questions.question: Can you create `new T()` inside a generic class?
options: No — type parameters can't be instantiated directly due to erasure
questions.answer: No — type parameters can't be instantiated directly due to erasure

questions.question: Can generics use primitive types like `Box<int>`?
options: No — use wrapper types like Box<Integer>
questions.answer: No — use wrapper types like Box<Integer>

questions.question: What is a raw type (e.g., `List list`)?
options: Using a generic type without specifying the type parameter — loses type safety
questions.answer: Using a generic type without specifying the type parameter — loses type safety

questions.question: What warning does using a raw type produce?
questions.answer: Unchecked or rawtypes warning

questions.question: What does `List<Object>` vs `List<String>` mean?
options: Not the same — generics are not covariant
questions.answer: Not the same — generics are not covariant

sublevels.theory:
**Bounded type parameters:**
```java
<T extends Comparable<T>>  // upper bound — T must be Comparable
<T extends Number>         // T must be Number or subtype
```

**Wildcards:**
```java
List<? extends Number> // covariant — read-only (produce)
List<? super Integer>  // contravariant — write-only (consume)
List<?>                // unbounded — read Object only
```

**PECS mnemonic:** Producer Extends, Consumer Super.

**Generic methods:** type params declared before return type:
```java
public <T extends Comparable<T>> T max(T a, T b) {
    return a.compareTo(b) > 0 ? a : b;
}
```

questions.question: What does `<T extends Comparable<T>>` mean?
options: T is a subclass of Object only
questions.answer: T must implement Comparable

questions.question: What is `List<? extends Number>` used for?
options: Read: accepts List<Integer>, List<Double>, etc. — read-only (upper bounded wildcard)
questions.answer: Read: accepts List<Integer>, List<Double>, etc. — read-only (upper bounded wildcard)

questions.question: What is the PECS principle?
options: Producer Extends, Consumer Super — guide for wildcard choice
questions.answer: Producer Extends, Consumer Super — guide for wildcard choice

questions.question: Can you `add` elements to `List<? extends Number>`?
options: No — upper bounded wildcards are effectively read-only for additions
questions.answer: No — upper bounded wildcards are effectively read-only for additions

questions.question: What does `List<? super Integer>` allow?
options: Adding Integer (or subtype) to the list; reading gives Object
questions.answer: Adding Integer (or subtype) to the list; reading gives Object

questions.question: Where do generic method type parameters go?
options: Before the return type: `public <T> T method(...)`
questions.answer: Before the return type: `public <T> T method(...)`

questions.question: What is `Collections.sort(List<T extends Comparable<T>>)` an example of?
options: A generic method with bounded type parameter
questions.answer: A generic method with bounded type parameter

questions.question: What is `Class<T>` used for?
options: Class metadata of type T — enables type-safe reflection operations
questions.answer: Class metadata of type T — enables type-safe reflection operations

questions.question: What is an intersection type `<T extends A & B>`?
options: T must be equal to A and B
T must implement both A and B
T is either A or B
questions.answer: T must implement both A and B

questions.question: Can a generic class extend a concrete class?
questions.answer: Yes, e.g., `class MyList<T> extends ArrayList<T>`

sublevels.theory:
**Heap pollution:** mixing raw and generic types can cause `ClassCastException` at a surprising location.

**Generic arrays:** `new T[]` is illegal — use `(T[]) new Object[n]` with unchecked warning, or `ArrayList<T>`.

**Reifiable vs non-reifiable types:** `List<String>` is non-reifiable (erased at runtime). `List` and `String` are reifiable.

**`TypeToken` pattern (Guava/Jackson):** capture generic type info at runtime via anonymous subclass:
```java
Type type = new TypeToken<List<String>>(){}.getType();
```

**`@SuppressWarnings("unchecked")`:** suppress unchecked cast warnings — use only when you know it's safe.

questions.question: What is heap pollution?
options: When a variable of parameterized type refers to an object of a different type — leads to ClassCastException
questions.answer: When a variable of parameterized type refers to an object of a different type — leads to ClassCastException

questions.question: Why can't you do `new T[n]` in Java?
options: T not available at runtime due to erasure
Arrays need exact type at runtime; T is erased
Both A and B — same reason
questions.answer: Both A and B — same reason

questions.question: What is a reifiable type?
options: A type with a generic parameter
A type whose full type info is available at runtime (not erased) — e.g., String, int[], List
questions.answer: A type whose full type info is available at runtime (not erased) — e.g., String, int[], List

questions.question: What does `@SafeVarargs` do?
options: Suppresses heap pollution warning for varargs generic methods that are type-safe
questions.answer: Suppresses heap pollution warning for varargs generic methods that are type-safe

questions.question: What is the `TypeToken` pattern?
options: A design pattern for abstract factories
Capturing parameterized type info at runtime using anonymous subclass of generic type
questions.answer: Capturing parameterized type info at runtime using anonymous subclass of generic type

questions.question: What does `isAssignableFrom` vs `instanceof` do?
options: `Class.isAssignableFrom(Class)` checks type hierarchy at reflective level; `instanceof` checks a concrete object at runtime
questions.answer: `Class.isAssignableFrom(Class)` checks type hierarchy at reflective level; `instanceof` checks a concrete object at runtime

questions.question: What is generic type capture?
options: When compiler captures the type of a wildcard in internal context to reason about it — 'capture#1 of ?'
Storing generic types in a map
questions.answer: When compiler captures the type of a wildcard in internal context to reason about it — 'capture#1 of ?'

questions.question: What does introducing sealed types help with in generic context?
options: Enables exhaustive pattern matching on sealed generic types — compiler knows all subtypes
questions.answer: Enables exhaustive pattern matching on sealed generic types — compiler knows all subtypes

questions.question: What is `Collections.checkedList(list, String.class)` for?
options: A dynamically type-checked list — throws ClassCastException at insertion if wrong type (useful with external raw-type code)
questions.answer: A dynamically type-checked list — throws ClassCastException at insertion if wrong type (useful with external raw-type code)

questions.question: What is Valhalla (Project Valhalla)?
options: JVM project to bring specialized generics over primitive types (int, etc.) — eliminating boxing overhead
questions.answer: JVM project to bring specialized generics over primitive types (int, etc.) — eliminating boxing overhead
